title: Frame Budgets, Profiling, Allocations, Collision Cost, and Responsiveness · Runtime Lens: Performance Regression
summary: The team optimizes by feel, reports average FPS, profiles menus instead of peak play, allocates every draw, rebuilds masks per frame, and changes physics for speed. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the performance regression increment in representative frame-time and capacity evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Gate percentile frame time, input-to-feedback latency, memory, load time, and semantic replay equivalence by target.
Measure candidate and narrow-phase pairs, entity caps, spatial index cost, and changed distribution cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict Performance Regression · Theory Performance Regression
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 2523 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-performance-regression — Gate percentile frame time, input-to-feedback latency, memory, load time, and semantic replay equivalence by target.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-performance-profiling-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-performance-profiling-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_performance_profiling_2(updates: int = 120, draws: int = 60, frame_p95_ms: float = 12.4, frame_budget_ms: float = 16.667, allocations_per_frame: int = 3, allocation_budget: int = 8, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if updates < 1 or draws < 1 or frame_p95_ms < 0.0:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "profile-domain"
    if frame_p95_ms > frame_budget_ms:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "frame-budget"
    if allocations_per_frame > allocation_budget:
        return False, allocations_per_frame, "allocation-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert frame_p95_ms <= frame_budget_ms
    return True, round(frame_p95_ms, 3), "profiled-frame"

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-performance-regression-qe8r9b
# Competency: asteroids-performance-regression.
# Case 7222: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def evidence_asteroids_performance_regression_qe8r9b(updates: int = 120, draws: int = 60, frame_p95_ms: float = 12.4, frame_budget_ms: float = 16.667, allocations_per_frame: int = 3, allocation_budget: int = 8, output_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_qe8r9b = "qe8r9b-b9r8eq-7222"
    assert evidence_variant_qe8r9b
    evidence_axes_qe8r9b = (
        "playerqe8r9b",
        "seed7222",
        "inputb9r8eq",
        "frameqe8r9b7222",
        "state7222qe8r9b",
        "playtestb9r8eq7222",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_qe8r9b)) == 6
    if updates < 1 or draws < 1 or frame_p95_ms < 0.0:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "profile-domain"
    if frame_p95_ms > frame_budget_ms:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "frame-budget"
    if allocations_per_frame > allocation_budget:
        return False, allocations_per_frame, "allocation-budget"
    if not output_equal:
        return False, frame_p95_ms, "semantic-regression"
    assert frame_p95_ms <= frame_budget_ms
    return True, round(frame_p95_ms, 3), "profiled-frame"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Performance Regression structural check is only one gate. representative frame-time and capacity evidence must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Build deterministic Python evidence to profile update and render phases against percentile frame-time, allocation, entity, collision, and responsiveness budgets without changing behavior; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Performance Regression model · Theory Performance Regre…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Performance Regression: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 3237 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to profile update and render phases against percentile frame-time, allocation, entity, collision, and responsiveness budgets without changing behavior; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate percentile frame time, input-to-feedback latency, memory, load time, and semantic replay equivalence by target.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Gate percentile frame time, input-to-feedback latency, memory, load time, and semantic replay equivalence by target.
